slides.title: 1. Polio (Poliomyelitis)
slides.description:
Cause: A Virus.

Spread: Drinking contaminated water or eating contaminated food.

Effect: It attacks the spinal cord and causes Paralysis (lameness) in the limbs.

Prevention: Immunisation with Polio Vaccine (Drops in the mouth). Drink boiled water.

slides.title: 2. Rickets & Osteomalacia
slides.description:
Cause: Deficiency (lack) of Vitamin D, Calcium, and Phosphorus.

Signs: Bow-legs (bending of legs), knock-knees, and weak bones.

Prevention: Eat foods rich in Calcium (Milk, Eggs) and expose the body to morning sunshine (Vitamin D).

slides.title: 3. Tuberculosis (TB) of the Spine
slides.description:
Cause: Bacteria (Mycobacterium).

Effect: It destroys the vertebrae (backbone), causing a painful hunchback (Kyphosis).

Prevention: Immunisation with BCG Vaccine at birth. Drinking boiled milk (to avoid bovine TB).

slides.title: 4. Tetanus (Lockjaw)
slides.description:
Cause: Bacteria (Clostridium tetani) found in soil.

Entry: Enters the body through fresh cuts or wounds (especially from rusty metal).

Signs: Stiff muscles, difficulty breathing, and lockjaw.

Prevention: DPT Vaccine. Cover wounds. Use sterilized tools.
